name: Meridian
description: A multi-tenant SaaS operations platform processing product events in real time — tenant dashboards, self-serve onboarding, and system health visibility for operations teams.
problem: Product and ops teams each maintained their own one-off dashboards, causing reporting fragmentation and slow response when customer health degraded.
solution: Built a shared platform with event-driven ingestion, tenant-scoped reporting views, and an operational health layer — one surface for all teams.
impact: Unified reporting across 15+ tenants, cut dashboard maintenance overhead, and gave ops teams real-time visibility into onboarding and system status.
features: Tenant-scoped analytics dashboards
Event ingestion pipeline via Kafka
Self-serve customer onboarding flow
Operational health cards and alerting
architecture: React dashboard renders tenant-filtered views with live data polling
Spring Boot REST API enforces tenant boundaries and owns reporting logic
Kafka pipeline handles async product event ingestion at scale
PostgreSQL stores normalized tenant, onboarding, and usage data

name: RecEngine
description: An AI-powered product recommendation microservice embedded in a Java backend — scores item relevance per user context and surfaces ranked lists through a REST API.
problem: Product listings were static and untargeted; teams needed personalized recommendations without adding a separate ML platform to maintain.
solution: Embedded OpenAI completions inside a Spring Boot microservice that builds context from user history and inventory, then returns ranked product lists through a clean API.
impact: Improved product listing accuracy by 40% in A/B testing; zero additional infrastructure cost by running inside existing Java services.
features: Context-aware OpenAI prompt assembly
Per-request ranking with score explanations
REST API for frontend and partner integrations
PostgreSQL query layer for user and inventory context
architecture: Spring Boot microservice owns recommendation request lifecycle
OpenAI API called with structured context built from DB query results
PostgreSQL stores user history, preferences, and product catalog
Redis caches ranked results to avoid redundant API calls for repeat queries

name: Prism UI
description: An accessible, themeable React component library built on Radix primitives — shared across product teams to replace one-off UI implementations.
problem: Teams were rebuilding the same buttons, modals, and forms in every product surface with inconsistent keyboard behavior and no shared accessibility baseline.
solution: Extracted a library of composable components from production code, wrapped Radix primitives for complex interactions, and published it as an internal npm package with Storybook docs.
impact: Reduced duplicated UI code across three product surfaces and gave teams a consistent accessibility and interaction baseline from day one.
features: Accessible primitives via Radix (dialog, popover, select)
Design token system for theming light and dark modes
Storybook documentation with interactive examples
TypeScript props API with strict variant typing
architecture: React components expose stable props API consumed by app teams
Radix primitives handle keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes
CSS variables drive theme tokens, overridable per product
Rollup bundles tree-shakeable ESM and CJS outputs

name: TrackBoard
description: A real-time logistics operations dashboard with live shipment status, WebSocket-driven updates, and interactive charts — built for operations teams managing high-volume order flows.
problem: Operations teams tracked shipment status through disconnected tools, causing delayed responses and manual status reconciliation during peak order periods.
solution: Built a single-page operations dashboard that streams live shipment events via WebSocket and visualizes order pipeline health with interactive charts.
impact: Reduced status reconciliation time for ops teams and gave supervisors live visibility into order pipeline bottlenecks without page refreshes.
features: Live shipment status via WebSocket stream
Order pipeline visualization with Chart.js
Alert panel for delayed or failed shipments
Filterable table with real-time row updates
architecture: React SPA manages dashboard state and chart lifecycle
WebSocket connection streams shipment events from backend
Firebase Realtime Database backs the event stream for low-latency delivery
Chart.js renders order pipeline metrics updated on each event

name: Conduit
description: A typed API gateway exposing REST and GraphQL surfaces for downstream services — with Redis-backed rate limiting and per-client request controls.
problem: Frontend teams were integrating against inconsistent backend contracts across several microservices, leading to over-fetching, SDK drift, and rate limit incidents.
solution: Built a Node.js gateway that normalises upstream service contracts into a single typed REST + GraphQL surface and enforces per-client rate limits via Redis.
impact: Reduced frontend integration drift and gave product teams a stable, predictable API contract with observable rate limit behavior.
features: REST and GraphQL gateway over existing microservices
Per-client rate limiting with Redis sliding window
Request logging and latency tracking via middleware
Environment-based client credential management
architecture: Node.js gateway normalises multiple upstream service contracts
GraphQL schema stitched over REST endpoints for product queries
Redis stores sliding window counters for per-client rate limits
Express middleware layer handles auth, logging, and error normalisation

name: Sentinel
description: A lightweight Go CLI that watches Kubernetes cluster events and routes filtered operational signals to Slack — without flooding channels with low-value noise.
problem: Engineering teams needed cluster event visibility but every existing solution either missed events or created alert fatigue by forwarding too much noise.
solution: Built a small Go CLI that connects to the Kubernetes event API, classifies events by severity and relevance, and sends concise Slack messages only for actionable signals.
impact: Teams gained reliable cluster visibility without Slack fatigue — critical events surfaced within seconds, routine churn silently dropped.
features: Kubernetes event stream via client-go watch API
Severity and namespace-based filtering rules
Slack block-kit message formatting per event type
Single YAML config for namespaces, filters, and routing
architecture: Go CLI connects to kube-apiserver via client-go with leader-election for HA
Event classifier maps reason codes to severity levels via config rules
Slack adapter constructs block-kit payloads and posts via webhook
Local config file controls watched namespaces and filter thresholds
